commit.message:
Implement the complete single-phase Valvatne-Blunt conductance model (#33)

* Implement Valvatne-Blunt single-phase conductance calculations and enhance shape factor methods

* Update conductance model to use 'valvatne_blunt' in benchmarks

* Add tests for Valvatne-Blunt conductance models and shape factor calculations

* Refactor conductance model references from 'valvatne_blunt_baseline' to 'valvatne_blunt' in tests

* Update conductance model in ipynbs to 'valvatne_blunt'

* Add MWE 14: Shape-factor hydraulic conductance comparison notebook

This notebook compares the default circular `generic_poiseuille` conductance with new shape-factor-aware Valvatne-Blunt models. It includes a synthetic Cartesian network and a real extracted Ketton rock network to evaluate the impact on absolute permeability estimation. Key findings include the recovery of the circular limit for throat-only shape-factor closure and the quantification of changes in throat conductance and absolute permeability for non-circular ducts.

* Update binary data files for voids network examples

* Update verification assets and enhance documentation for single-phase conductance models

- Updated binary images for permeability and porosity scatter plots.
- Modified CSV files for Stokes case results and limit representative data to reflect new calculations.
- Expanded background documentation with detailed explanations of the equivalent duct concept and shape-aware conductance models.
- Clarified hydraulic geometry fields and their significance in single-phase conductance models in the concepts documentation.
- Revised verification documentation to reflect the transition from `valvatne_blunt_baseline` to the complete `valvatne_blunt` model.

* Enhance tests for valvatne_blunt conductance model with additional validations and shape factor calculations

* Update section title from 'voids' to 'Overview' in documentation

* Add geometry repair options for Imperial export in network extraction and PoreSpy import

* Update docs

* Refactor tests: remove obsolete PoreSpy import tests and add new tests for Imperial export repairs

* Add external pnflow benchmark notebook for voids comparison

This notebook compares the `voids` workflow against a committed reference dataset produced with the Imperial College `pnextract` and `pnflow` workflow. It includes functionality to load reference data, run the `voids` extraction, and generate comparative metrics and visualizations for permeability and porosity. The results are saved for documentation and review.

* Refactor benchmark notebook: add project root detection, save figures, and improve plotting structure

* Add verification reports for OpenPNM cross-check and external pnextract/pnflow benchmark, including new figures and CSV results

* Validate shape_factor and throat area before overriding area in porespy importer (#34)

* Initial plan

* Validate shape_factor and throat area before overriding area in porespy importer

Fix cable trench mid-segment entry/exit backtracking (#5)

* Fix cable trench entry/exit backtracking by trying both endpoints

When a point enters/exits a cable trench at a mid-segment foot point,
the old algorithm used a direction hint to pick one trench endpoint.
This could cause backtracks when Dijkstra later routed through the
other endpoint.

The fix adds PlanBestSegment() which tries all 4 combinations of
source/dest trench endpoints (2x2) and picks the shortest total route.
This eliminates backtracking at both entry and exit points.

Also adds 6 regression tests with sanitized real-world coordinates.

commit.message:
WIP: Add TilePanel and FloatPanel widgets (#201)

* Add FloatPanel and TilePanel widgets with TileDemo sample

FloatPanel: General-purpose absolute positioning container where children
float at (x, y) character coordinates. Widget order implies z-order.
Supports clipping, focus traversal, and input routing.

TilePanel: Composite widget for infinite pan/zoom over tile-based data.
Composes SurfaceWidget (tiles) + FloatPanel (POIs) + ZStack (overlays).
- ITileDataSource interface for async tile fetching
- TilePointOfInterest for map markers as IconWidgets
- Integer zoom levels (1x, 2x, 4x)
- Configurable input bindings (arrows=pan, +/-=zoom)
- Event-driven camera (OnPan, OnZoom, OnPoiClicked)

TileDemo: Sample app with checkerboard data source and POIs.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Wrap SurfaceWidget in Interactable for proper focus/input/mouse support

- TilePanelNode no longer overrides IsFocusable/IsFocused/GetFocusableNodes
- Instead, BuildContent wraps the SurfaceWidget in an InteractableWidget
- Input bindings (pan, zoom, drag) attached via .WithInputBindings()
- InteractableNode provides correct focus, hit-testing, and bounds
- Fixed HeightHint=Fill via extension methods so VStack gives proper bounds
- Root cause of mouse not working: VStack gave int.MaxValue height to
  content-sized TilePanel, causing HitTest bounds overflow
- Added OemPlus/OemMinus key bindings alongside numpad Add/Subtract
- Added 3 integration tests: keyboard pan, keyboard zoom, mouse scroll zoom

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Replace checkerboard TileDemo with OpenStreetMap raster tile renderer

Implements a real map viewer using half-block Unicode characters (▀▄█) for
1×2 sub-pixel resolution. Downloads PNG tiles from tile.openstreetmap.org
and converts pixel pairs to colored terminal characters.

Well-factored into focused files:
- TileCoordinates.cs: Slippy map lat/lon ↔ tile coordinate math
- MapCamera.cs: Camera state with character-space pan/zoom
- HalfBlockRenderer.cs: PNG pixel → half-block character conversion
- RasterTileClient.cs: HTTP client with memory LRU + disk tile caching
- OsmTileDataSource.cs: ITileDataSource bridging tiles to TilePanel
- Program.cs: Thin entry point with NYC landmarks as POIs

Uses TileSize=(1,1) so each TilePanel cell = one character with
independent fg/bg colors. Camera maps to OSM zoom levels 0-18.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix TilePanel public API surface

- EventArgs now extend WidgetEventArgs<TilePanelWidget, TilePanelNode>,
  gaining CancellationToken, Popups, Notifications, Windows, and
  PushAnchored convenience accessors
- Make PanCallback, ZoomCallback, PoiClickedCallback internal on
  TilePanelNode — these are reconciliation plumbing, not consumer API
- Make EffectiveTileWidth/Height internal — zoom math implementation detail
- Make DataSource/PointsOfInterest setters internal on TilePanelNode —
  set by reconciliation, read-only for consumers

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add zoom-toward-mouse support to TilePanel

When zooming via mouse scroll, the map now zooms toward the cursor
position rather than always zooming toward the viewport center.

- Add PivotX/PivotY to TilePanelZoomEventArgs (tile-space coords of
  the zoom target: cursor position for mouse, viewport center for keyboard)
- TilePanelNode.HandleZoom computes pivot from InputBindingActionContext
  mouse coordinates when available
- TileDemo converts pivot through lat/lon to maintain the geographic
  point under the cursor across zoom level changes

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Adopt ActionId keybinding infrastructure for TilePanelWidget

Add rebindable ActionId constants (PanUp, PanDown, PanLeft, PanRight,
PanUpFast, PanDownFast, PanLeftFast, PanRightFast, ZoomIn, ZoomOut,
ResetPosition) to TilePanelWidget and migrate TilePanelNode bindings
from .Action() to .Triggers() to align with the new keybinding
rebinding infrastructure introduced in #223.

OemPlus/OemMinus keys use rebind-only .Triggers(ActionId) to share
the ZoomIn/ZoomOut handlers registered on the numpad keys.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix +/- keys not working for TilePanel zoom on Linux terminals

ParseKeyInput mapped raw punctuation characters like +, -, =, etc. to
Hex1bKey.None, so key bindings for OemPlus/OemMinus never matched on
terminals that send raw bytes (i.e., all Linux terminals).

Add explicit character-to-Hex1bKey mappings for common punctuation in
both Hex1bTerminal and Hex1bAppWorkloadAdapter ParseKeyInput methods.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
🔒 Fix Command Injection in Git Route (#111)

* 🔒 Fix command injection vulnerability in git route

- Replaced child_process.exec with execFile to prevent shell string interpolation in `/v1/git/run`.
- Implemented a strict whitelist of allowed git commands mapped to explicit argument arrays.
- Removed the "Custom command" functionality from both the backend API and the frontend `GitCommandsModal.tsx` UI to ensure no user-provided strings can trigger arbitrary commands.
- Rejected unauthorized commands with a 400 error status.

Co-authored-by: RSBalchII <128771311+RSBalchII@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update engine/src/routes/v1/git.ts

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Signed-off-by: RSBalchII <128771311+RSBalchII@users.noreply.github.com>

* 🔒 Fix command injection vulnerability in git route

- Replaced child_process.exec with execFile to prevent shell string interpolation in `/v1/git/run`.
- Implemented a strict whitelist of allowed git commands mapped to explicit argument arrays.
- Removed the "Custom command" functionality from both the backend API and the frontend `GitCommandsModal.tsx` UI to ensure no user-provided strings can trigger arbitrary commands.
- Rejected unauthorized commands with a 400 error status.

Co-authored-by: RSBalchII <128771311+RSBalchII@users.noreply.github.com>

* 🔒 Fix command injection and unauthorized directory access in git route

- Replaced `child_process.exec` with `execFile` to prevent shell string interpolation in `/v1/git/run`.
- Implemented a strict whitelist mapping frontend commands to explicit `git` argument arrays.
- Removed "Custom command" functionality from both the backend API and frontend `GitCommandsModal.tsx` UI to ensure no user-provided strings can trigger arbitrary commands.
- Implemented `working_dir` validation by explicitly discovering allowed repository paths (`getDiscoveredRepos()`) and verifying the requested absolute path against this whitelist, preventing path traversal and unauthorized filesystem execution.
- Rejected unauthorized commands and unauthorized directories with 400 and 403 error statuses, respectively.

Co-authored-by: RSBalchII <128771311+RSBalchII@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: use cmd.Process.Wait() to avoid pipe race in octool exec (#1354)

`TestManager_MaxLinesTrimsOutput` fails flakily in CI: expects `"c"`
(last line after max-lines trim) but gets `"a"` (first line, i.e.
truncated output).

### Root cause

`cmd.Wait()` closes the pipe read ends returned by
`StdoutPipe`/`StderrPipe` immediately after the process exits, racing
with `readFrom` goroutines still draining those pipes. Per Go docs: *"It
is thus incorrect to call Wait before all reads from the pipe have
completed."*

### Fix

Replace `cmd.Wait()` with `cmd.Process.Wait()` in the background session
goroutine — waits for process exit without closing pipes. The IO
goroutines get EOF naturally when the OS closes the child's write ends.

```go
// Before: cmd.Wait() closes pipe read ends, racing with readFrom goroutines
err := cmd.Wait()
waitDone(sess.ioDone, defaultIODrain)
sess.markDone(exitCode(err))

// After: Process.Wait() only reaps the process, pipes stay open for readers
ps, _ := cmd.Process.Wait()
waitDone(sess.ioDone, defaultIODrain)
code := -1
if ps != nil {
    code = ps.ExitCode()
}
sess.markDone(code)
```

### Documentation changes (also in this PR)

- Renamed openclaw docs from "Demo" to "Implementation" throughout
- Added `openclaw/README.zh_CN.md` Chinese translation
